questions: I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. My doctor just confirmed my hearing loss from the accident is permanent. My partner asked how the appointment went.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. The company I spent eight years building just folded and can't be revived. My closest friend called to check in.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. My ex ended things for good six months ago and has moved on. My sister asked how I'm doing these days.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. My appeal for the job position I wanted was rejected and the role is filled. A friend asked how the job search is going.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. The chronic pain condition I was diagnosed with two years ago has not improved at all. My coworker asked how I've been feeling.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. My childhood home was demolished last month — it's gone. My brother texted asking what's new.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. The grant I applied for was awarded to someone else and there's no reapplication opportunity this cycle. My research partner asked what I'm thinking about the project now.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. My father passed away eight months ago. My mom called and asked how I've been holding up.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. The visa denial was final — the life I planned in another country is officially not happening. A college friend texted asking what I'm up to these days.

I refuse to accept things I can't change — I keep fighting and pushing no matter what anyone tells me, because I believe if I just try hard enough I can reverse any outcome. The relationship I had with my estranged parent will never be what I wanted — she died last year without reconciling. My therapist asked what I've been sitting with.
